channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Django 5.2 release touts automatic model importing
item.description: The latest version of the Python web framework features an automatic model-importing capability in the shell. This release also spells the end of support levels for previous version 5.x releases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Asteroid 2024 YR4: NASA's Webb Space Telescope Captures Images of Potentially Hazardous Asteroid
item.description: NASA and the European Space Agency release images of asteroid 2024 YR4, which had a 3% chance of hitting Earth in 2032. The Webb Space Telescope confirms the asteroid is nearly 200 feet across.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: United Airlines to Offer High-Speed In-Flight Wi-Fi with Starlink
item.description: United Airlines announces approval from the Federal Aviation Administration to equip its aircraft with high-speed Starlink Wi-Fi, allowing passengers to stay connected during flights.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump launches new office to oversee CHIPS Act, boost semiconductor investments
item.description: US President Donald J. Trump signed an executive order this week creating the US Investment Accelerator office to oversee the CHIPS Act, a Biden-era program to re-shore semiconductor production.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vapes aren’t just for smoking anymore—now they’re digital toys
item.description: Vaping devices are getting a makeover—one that seems likely to turn more teens into consumers. Experts have long worried that nicotine vapes appeal to minors with flavors like Cotton Candy and Blue Razz, along with bright candy-colored designs. Today’s vapes double as digital gadgets.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Underground trading of malicious LLMs is fueling cybercrime
item.description: The web is being swamped by AI slop—but the swamp is creeping closer to home. Your email inboxes, phone SMS apps, instant messaging, and social media services are all being overtaken by inauthentic content.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 mouse mode revealed: Joy Con 2 mouse function, how it works, and which games are confirmed to use it
item.description: The Nintendo Switch 2 Direct livestream has confirmed and expanded previous rumors on the Joy Con 2’s mouse functionality, showcasing games that make the most of the new control mode.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Leadership, trust and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: As the military navigates the complexities of the 21st-century battlefield, the integration of artificial intelligence is a leadership imperative. The post Leadership, trust and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple TV+ has discovered the Fountain of Youth and you can jump in this May when the movie starts streaming
item.description: Fountain of Youth is an epic new adventure coming to Apple TV+ directed by Guy Ritchie and comes from the writer of Scream.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: FTC chair defends Trump firing of Democratic commissioners
item.description: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Andrew Ferguson on Wednesday defended President Trump’s decision to fire the agency’s Democratic commissioners.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Say goodbye to disruptive OLED Care prompts on monitors - MSI will extend OLED Care 2.0 function to 24 hours
item.description: OLED monitors are gradually becoming more accessible for budget PC users, and MSI's efforts on its OLED Care 2.0 technology is great to see.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why no one is challenging Trump’s executive order that keeps TikTok running
item.description: After TikTok was banned in the United States earlier this year, President Donald Trump gave the platform a reprieve, barreling past a law that was passed in Congress and upheld unanimously by the Supreme Court.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: NetBox Labs launches drift detection tool to tame configuration chaos
item.description: NetBox Labs this week made available a new product that will help network teams detect and remediate configuration drift across sophisticated network environments before costly service disruptions or unplanned downtime affect services or applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Case in point: taking stock of the CrowdStrike outages
item.description: Last summer, a faulty CrowdStrike software update took down millions of computers, caused billions in damages, and underscored that companies are still not able to manage third-party risks, or respond quickly and efficiently to disruptions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Rewriting Social Security will be a train wreck
item.description: Government software development is a curious business. When I was a student at the Naval Postgraduate School, I wrote a paper called “There is a Lot of Money to be Made Writing Bad Software for the Federal Government.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk blames Tesla protests on shady payoffs—all while he makes shady payoffs in Wisconsin
item.description: After yet another round of protests disrupted hundreds of Tesla dealerships over the weekend, Elon Musk, the carmaker’s CEO/presidential sidekick, fumed on the social media platform he owns.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Nasdaq 100’s worst quarter in years sealed by AI bubble fears
item.description: In a quarter marred by tariff uncertainty, US government spending cuts and the threat of recession, it is fears about a bubble brewing in artificial intelligence that have dealt the latest blow to the Nasdaq 100.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Modern app delivery requires a continuous approach to security
item.description: At the Tanzu Division of Broadcom, we focus on how our customers can get the most out of cloud native environments while protecting against the slew of new vulnerabilities and attacks targeting their critical business apps.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lightmatter launches photonic chips to eliminate GPU idle time in AI data centers
item.description: Lightmatter has announced new silicon photonics products that could dramatically speed up AI systems by solving a critical problem: the sluggish connections between AI chips in data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Agentic AI won’t make public cloud providers rich
item.description: While major cloud providers, such as AWS, Google, and Microsoft are rushing to position themselves as leaders in AI development and deployment, the reality of agentic AI’s impact on public cloud growth may not align with their ambitious projections—at least, in my opinion. I’ll tell you why.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon, OpenAI, and China’s Zhipu unveil new AI tools amid intensifying competition
item.description: A wave of new AI products is hitting the market, signaling a shift toward more autonomous, task-completing systems that could reshape how businesses and consumers interact with digital services. Amazon has unveiled Nova Act, an AI agent designed to operate a web browser much like a human user.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Volcano erupts in southwestern Iceland after a nearby town and spa are evacuated
item.description: A volcano in southwestern Iceland that has erupted repeatedly for more than a year again belched lava and smoke into the air, just hours after authorities evacuated the few remaining residents of a nearby fishing village.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk and conservative influencer Ashley St. Clair publicly feuded on the social platform X on Monday after she accused the world’s richest person of withdrawing child care support and refusing to take a paternity test.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: After fake employees, fake enterprises are next hiring threat to corporate data
item.description: Chinese companies are trying to cut Taiwan’s lead in semiconductor technology by hiring away its best engineering talent through ‘front’ companies that hide their connections to China, the Taiwanese Ministry of Justice Investigation Bureau (MJIB) has alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Jack Black, Jason Momoa enter the Minecraft universe as the video game phenom hits the big screen
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Jack Black got the call to star in the “Minecraft” movie, the perks were plenty. He got to work alongside Jason Momoa, reunite with “Nacho Libre” director Jared Hess and engulf himself into a beloved gaming universe.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google introduces Gemini 2.5 reasoning models
item.description: Google has introduced version 2.5 of its Gemini AI model, which the company said offers a new level of performance by combining an enhanced base model with improved post-training.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ECMAScript 2025 JavaScript standard takes shape
item.description: ECMAScript 2025, the next version of an ECMA International standard for JavaScript, will standardize new JavaScript capabilities ranging from JSON modules to import attributes, new Set methods, sync iterator helpers, and regular expression modifiers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Airtel connects India with 100Tbps submarine cable
item.description: In a significant development for digital infrastructure in India, Bharti Airtel has landed the 2Africa Pearls submarine cable, delivering 100 Terabits per second of international network capacity for the country. The submarine cable will connect to Airtel’s cable landing station (CLS) in Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Teams successful on 52.2% of ball/strike challenges during spring training robot umpire test
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Teams won 52.2% of their ball/strike challenges during the spring training test of robot umpires, and catchers had a markedly better success rate than pitchers.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation
